name: conductor-board-bootstrap
description: Set up the board, convert a skill into cards, map dependencies, and execute it.

steps.instruction:
Verify the environment can run conductor-board.
Check that Node.js 18+ and npx are available.
Note: the supported one-command path is `conductor-board run SKILL.md`, which does compile-if-needed, opens the board, and dispatches the run end to end (see board/START.md). The steps below are the expanded, drive-by-hand form of that same flow.
steps.title: Preflight

steps.instruction:
Reserve the board for this run. Do not execute workflow cards yet.
The visible board will be initialized after the workflow is generated and
validated, using conductor-board init-board so it can confirm the current
workflow is live before execution starts.
steps.title: Reserve Board

steps.instruction:
Compile or reuse the accepted workflow skeleton for this skill:
  conductor-board compile --skill SKILL.md
If no accepted compiled workflow exists, this command must create checked cards, map checked dependencies, spot-audit the graph, validate the workflow, and save the accepted skeleton for future runs. If an accepted skeleton exists for the same skill/compiler version, reuse it. Do not manually create .conductor/cards.json or .conductor/workflow.json unless compile fails.
steps.title: Compile Workflow

steps.instruction:
Confirm compile produced the reusable skeleton files:
  test -f .conductor/cards.json && test -f .conductor/workflow.json && test -f .conductor/compile-meta.json
Read .conductor/compile-meta.json and note whether this was a first compile or accepted cache reuse. Do not redo card design or dependency mapping if compile succeeded.
steps.title: Confirm Compile

steps.instruction:
Validate the generated workflow before anything executes:
  conductor-board validate .conductor/workflow.json
Fix every validation error. Do not proceed until validation passes.
steps.title: Validate Workflow

steps.instruction:
Initialize the visible board for the current workflow before any card execution:
  conductor-board init-board .conductor/workflow.json
This command must run status-init, start or reuse the board server, open the
browser, confirm /health is live, and confirm the board is serving the current
workflow name. Do not proceed unless init-board prints the board URL and success.
steps.title: Initialize Visible Board

steps.instruction: Before executing, judge the visible board as a veteran flow manager would. Can you see the whole story? Is every card independently checkable? Is the grouping one altitude? Do the titles read like promises? Are the instructions concrete enough for an independent checker to compare output against them? Confirm every card can produce a durable artifact under .conductor/artifacts/: work product for creation cards, action record for action cards, and markdown receipt for non-text work. Image cards must embed every produced image inline in the receipt. complete refuses Done without one. Fix every disappointment before running.
steps.title: Review Board

steps.instruction:
Execute the generated conductor workflow.
Do not begin card execution until init-board confirms the board is live and showing the current workflow.
Phase 0 self-improvement is parked in v3 and off by default. If a conductor
explicitly sets auto_improve: true, the old improvement cards may be injected,
but normal runs should start the work cards directly.
Set the top-level "goal" from the conductor's description, and refresh
"current_step_goal" each time current_step changes.
Walk each step in order, updating status.json after EVERY transition
(pending -> running -> checking -> passed/failed -> done). The human is
watching the board to follow along — never do real work without updating the
board first. Doing work the board doesn't reflect ("freeballing") is not
allowed: if you drift, stop, re-sync the board, restart the step cleanly, and
apologize. The board shows a red "Freeballing?" banner after ~3 minutes
without an agent update. Retry on checker failure — never skip.
NOTE: this step runs only after review-board, so the board is already one a
flow manager would trust before any real work starts.
Write progress updates with conductor-board update <step> "note". The old
heartbeat command remains a compatibility alias, but product language is
update. Treat each update like a Codex preamble: concise, grouped, and useful
for bringing the user along while work is happening.
Do not write one update for every tiny read, command, or edit. Group related
actions. Keep updates to 1-2 sentences; for quick updates, aim for 8-12 words.
Build on prior context: say what you learned so far and what it implies for the
next action. Avoid trivial updates unless they are part of a larger grouped
action. Never write status-log updates like "drafting hero", "checking",
"running tests", or "checker passed" — the board already shows system status.
Good update: "README still describes explicit gates, so I am rewriting the
quick start around instruction-based checking."
Good update: "Choosing verified cards over gates because v3 no longer has gate
fields."
Before marking each step done, append a final update with --handoff --to
<next-step> that summarizes what the next card needs to know. Store the actual
work product or action record in .conductor/artifacts/<card-index>-<slugified-card-title>.md; updates are
narration, not the work product. For non-text work, write a human-readable markdown receipt as the artifact and
reference supporting files inside it. For image work, embed every image produced
by that card inline in the receipt with markdown image syntax, for example
![Alt text](image.webp). The artifact must include concrete proof: command/script, inputs, return value,
changed resource and verification result when applicable.
For loop steps, update "completed" and the "iterations" object as EACH
iteration finishes — don't wait until the loop ends.
At the START of the run, read .conductor/insights.md (if it exists) to carry
forward what past runs learned — don't repeat insights already recorded there.
At run end, before setting status "done", write what you learned into the
conductor's knowledge section — the conductor IS the knowledge base. Use:
  conductor-board suggest "title" --scope <scope> [--step S --current X --proposed Y]
--scope is REQUIRED (this-conductor | upstream | template | tooling | corpus).
A repeat sighting escalates emerging -> proven (3x); proven this-conductor
insights auto-apply in the next run's Phase 0. Browse them on the board's
Insights page. Set the top-level status to "done" when the last step
completes.
steps.title: Execute Workflow
